title: inula conyza subsp. gutierrezii
intro: Inula conyza subsp. gutierrezii, also known as Iberis or Wild Candytuft, is a flowering plant species native to the Iberian Peninsula. The medicinal properties of Iberis lie in its ability to soothe and calm the digestive system, and it has been traditionally used to treat conditions such as indigestion and bloating. The health benefits of Iberis include its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help to reduce inflammation and protect against cell damage. Iberis can be prepared as a tea, infused oil, or tincture, and is often used in herbal remedies to promote digestive health and well-being.
